body.title: CLOSED: CC chat log Handler+Formatter class in agent_datacenter
body.result: ChatLogHandler+CCEventFormatter in devices/claude/chat_log_handler.py; ClaudeDevice.logs() returns CC.0 path; 27 tests; pushed abc6398
body.description:
CC's JSONL transcript → markdown pipeline is currently two bespoke standalone scripts (export_chat.py, chat_log_formatter.py) that bypass the agent_datacenter logging interface. A proper logging.Handler + logging.Formatter subclass in agent_datacenter would make CC a first-class device in the log system.

Proposed shape: new devices/claude/chat_log_handler.py — a logging.Handler subclass with embedded Formatter. Accepts CC JSONL event dicts (user/assistant/tool_use/tool_result entries), writes date-partitioned markdown to AGENT_DATACENTER_HOME/logs/CC.0/YYYY-MM-DD.md. Same output format as export_chat.py (### User — / ### Assistant — with full datetime). ClaudeDevice.logs() added to return the CC.0 log path (implementing BaseDevice contract). NOT modifying LoggingControlCenter — it already names CC.0 in its tree layout.

**Affected files:** devices/claude/chat_log_handler.py (new), devices/claude/device.py (add logs()), tests/test_cc_log_handler.py (new)
**Design rules:** no-sqlite (n/a)
**Scope boundary:** Handler + Formatter class + ClaudeDevice.logs(). Not modifying LoggingControlCenter. Not changing Stop hook wiring (that's T-cc-log-stop-hook-wire).
**Test plan:** unit tests in tests/test_cc_log_handler.py — verify markdown output format matches export_chat.py output, date partitioning routes messages to correct day file, user/assistant/tool entries render correctly, missing timestamp handled gracefully.
**Pass condition:** ChatLogHandler.emit(event_dict) on a sample JSONL user+assistant turn produces markdown matching export_chat.py's ### User / ### Assistant format in the correct dated output file.
